pr.title: output calcprogress to console if map output enabled
pr.author: EpochFlame

summary: Small tooling quality-of-life PR that runs tools/calcprogress.py automatically after DOL conversion when GENERATE_MAP=1. The script paths were adjusted from parent-directory-relative paths to repository-root-relative paths so it can be invoked from the Makefile in the normal build context.

key_files.reason: Added conditional invocation of tools/calcprogress.py after converting an ELF to a DOL when GENERATE_MAP is enabled.
Changed DOL_PATH and MAP_PATH to be relative to the repository root rather than the tools directory's parent-relative assumptions.

what_changed: In the %.dol build rule, after running $(ELF2DOL), the Makefile now checks ifeq ($(GENERATE_MAP),1) and invokes $(PYTHON) tools/calcprogress.py $@.
The PR body describes the change as a quality-of-life improvement.
smart_moves: Gated the progress output behind the existing GENERATE_MAP flag, so normal builds are not forced to run progress reporting unless map output is enabled.
Integrated progress reporting into the existing DOL conversion rule rather than requiring a separate manual command.
Used existing Makefile variables $(QUIET) and $(PYTHON), preserving build-system conventions for verbosity and Python invocation.
decomp_lessons: Progress-report tooling is most useful when connected to existing build outputs such as the generated .map file, but should be conditional to avoid adding unnecessary noise or cost to every build.
When a Python tool is invoked from the repository Makefile, its default file paths should match the Makefile's working directory assumptions, usually the repository root.
Small quality-of-life automation can improve decomp workflow by making match-progress feedback appear as part of regular map-generating builds.
naming_conventions: The Makefile uses the existing uppercase GENERATE_MAP flag to control optional map/progress behavior.
The progress script keeps uppercase module constants such as DOL_PATH, MAP_PATH, MEM1_HI, and MEM1_LO.
The generated map path in this slice follows build/ssbm.us.1.2/GALE01.map.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No direct assembly matching tactic is present; this PR only improves build-time progress reporting.
The relevant decomp workflow tactic is to compute progress from the current DOL and generated map after DOL conversion when map generation is enabled.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
follow_up_queries: Find later changes to tools/calcprogress.py path handling or argument parsing.
Find PRs that modify GENERATE_MAP behavior in the Makefile.
Find PRs that integrate decomp progress reporting into build or CI tooling.
